reforms.description: The Icelandic government introduced catch quotas for individual vessels to distribute fishing rights more equitably and encourage the retirement of less efficient boats.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By internalizing externality costs and promoting efficiency, the reform aims to enhance the sustainability and productivity of the fishing industry.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Fisheries introduced catch quotas for individual vessels in early 1984.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in fisheries management, aiming to improve efficiency and sustainability in a critical sector of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: An important innovation in fisheries management was introduced when the Ministry of Fisheries in early 1984 introduced catch quotas for individual vessels, rather than overall quotas for the industry.
